title: Container Creation, Process Lifecycle, CLI State, and Cleanup · Docker Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A release board compares fresh container creation, process lifecycle, cli state, and cleanup decisions and accepts only answers tied to observable image, container, build, or Compose evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for deterministic container lifecycle runbook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish create, run, start, attach, exec, stop, kill, restart, pause, wait, remove, and auto-remove state transitions.
Apply names, environment, working directory, user, entrypoint, command, labels, hosts, and platform overrides with correct precedence.
Choose foreground, detached, interactive, TTY, attach, and log behavior while preserving standard streams and exit evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:
